meaning: adj.不合格的,不胜任的n.无能力者
examples: He wants the power to fire incompetent employees.
I don't think she's correct to say he's incompetent.
The man was a fool, he thought, or at least incompetent.
Spain will feel betrayed and will see the EU as inflexible, incompetent and vindictive.
"Freud wasn't working, it was incompetent, so I looked around for something better, " he says.
